observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to:
strengthen the internal control system in hiring of additional workforce through job/order/contract of service.   Limit the hiring of JOs per Office or to the actual number needed for normal operations, taking into consideration the volume of transactions per Office; and
instruct the JO employees to include quantifiable date in their accomplishment report such as number of tasks completed, clients served, documents processed, inspections conducted, reports prepared, or any other relevant performance;
Direct all JO employees to quantify all the accomplishments in the accomplishment reports;
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to liquidate the cash advances for payment of JO services within five (5) days after each fifteen (15)-day or end-of-month pay period; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant - Designate to ensure the validity and propriety of the supporting documents attached to the payroll.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant - Designate to:
prepare necessary journal entry to reflect the correct balances of the related liability accounts.
ensure that accounting entries for the withholding and remittance of mandatory deductions are correct and posted in the General Ledger and reflected in the financial statements; and
ensure that the computation of contributions is adherent to the circular issued by Pag-IBIG.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Engineer to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers, contracts, and their supporting documents.
Henceforth, the Municipal Mayor to implement a control system to ensure all disbursement vouchers and supporting documents are submitted regularly and in a timely manner, aligning with standard accounting practices and regulatory requirements.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor impose appropriate sanctions to the municipal officials concerned, such as suspension of the payment of salary and other emoluments, and administrative disciplinary action, among others, if warranted, for their continued disregard of the law, rules and regulation.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman and the Municipal Engineer to:
submit the six infrastructure contracts with a contract price totaling ₱11,909,042.00; and
ensure that contracts are submitted to the Audit Team within five working days from their perfection.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal DRRM Officer to:
	i.	refrain from utilizing the fund for non-disaster related programs, projects and activities;
	ii.	prepare a revised LDRRMFIP whenever there are changes in the allocation of the DRRM Fund; and

b.	Municipal Accountant - Designate and Municipal DRRM Officer to ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete supporting documents.  Furthermore, utilization of the fund is properly reported and disclosed.
Municipal Budget Officer to stop the charging of ineligible expenses that cannot be appropriately categorized under any of the accepted disaster-related expenditures to the LDRRM Fund.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
a. 	Direct the creation of the GAD Focal Point System, including the TWG and/or Secretariat;
b. 	Issue amendments or a new Executive Order, as necessary, to incorporate changes, such as changes to the GFPS membership, and ensure that members are active and serving; and
c. 	Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to prepare and submit the GAD Utilization Report and the Registries of Appropriations and Obligations to the Audit Team in a timely manner.

We reiterate that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Engineer to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers, contracts, and their supporting documents.  
Henceforth, the Municipal Mayor to implement a control system to ensure all disbursement vouchers and supporting documents are submitted regularly and in a timely manner, aligning with standard accounting practices and regulatory requirements.
We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor impose appropriate sanctions to the municipal officials concerned, such as suspension of the payment of salary and other emoluments, and administrative disciplinary action, among others, if warranted, for their continued disregard of the law, rules and regulation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to:
Draw a journal entry voucher to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment/correction in the books and the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees regarding the existence of stale checks for their cancellation and replacement;
Exert extra effort to account for the difference of ₱448,726.03 between the book and bank balances in prior years, and adjust the books accordingly; and
Prepare and submit the monthly BRS together with all the necessary supporting documents for all bank depository accounts maintained by the LGU within ten days from receipt of the BS.
The Municipal Accountant commented that the BRS were already transmitted to COA, however, she accepted the audit observation and committed to comply with the recommendations.
The Audit Team emphasized that based on the office records, submitted BRS pertained only to the GF, and no submission for the SEF and TF.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Engineer to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers, contracts, and their supporting documents.
Henceforth, ensure that all disbursement vouchers, and their pertinent supporting documents be submitted on a regular and timely basis.
The representative of the Acting Municipal Treasurer explained that the DVs were not submitted because the Treasurer’s Office is still getting the signatures of the officials concerned.  The Municipal Accountant-Designate added that she had already conferred with the Acting Municipal Treasurer to resolve the recurring problem about the non-submission of accounts.  She also informed the Audit Team that the DVs are already for transmittal.
The Audit Team emphasized that the CY 2021 accounts are already overdue since they should be transmitted within ten days of the ensuing month, thus, the DVs and their supporting documents should be transmitted immediately to COA.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct:
Municipal Budget Officer to:
properly record the obligated expenditures;
reconcile the RAAO and SAAOB; and
reconcile with the reports prepared by the Municipal Accountant-Designate
Municipal Accountant – Designate to:
record the utilization properly in the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization, and submit the same on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development (LDC) to the COA auditor of the Municipality;
reconcile the Notes to Financial Statements and Monthly Sources and Utilization Report;
reconcile the reports prepared by Budget Officer; and
prepare an adjusting entry for the recording of the transfer.
Municipal Treasurer to transfer immediately the cash corresponding to the unexpended balance as of year-end of LDRRMF for QRF and MOOE from General Fund to Trust Fund; and
Management that Local DRRM Fund be appropriately utilized for disaster-related activities as set forth under COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
The Municipal Accountant commented that the Accounting Office prepares entry for the transfer of funds from the GF to TF only when there is an actual transfer.
The Audit Team explained that even though there is no actual transfer of funds for the unexpended MF-MOOE and QRF, there is still a need to record the amount of funds that should be transferred to the Special Trust Fund account as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit the aforementioned contracts and pertinent documents for evaluation and review.  Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-01.
The BAC Chairman explained that the management is not really aware that contracts are to be submitted within five calendar days from their perfection.  The management thought that the contracts and the pertinent documents are to be submitted when a claim for the billings is made.  Moreover, the Municipal Engineer also commented that the restrictions of the pandemic caused the non-submission of the same.
The Audit Team reiterated that the legal deadline for the submission of government contracts is within five calendar days from their perfection.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the required supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers identified in Annex A and to ensure that all disbursement vouchers and obligation requests are properly approved, and the disbursement vouchers and their pertinent supporting documents are submitted on a regular and timely basis.
The Municipal Accountant reasoned out that the supporting documents for the monetization of leave credits were already attached with the DV covering the partial payment.  She also explained that the payments for the catering services were supported with a certification, instead of acceptance and inspection report; and the distribution of the rice was acknowledged by the punong barangays when the sacks of rice were distributed, and the documentation of the eventual distribution was also made at the barangay level.
The Audit Team emphasized that all disbursement should be supported with complete documentation, and approved by the officials concerned.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit immediately the subsidiary ledgers and supporting schedules;
Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant-Designate to devise a process that will eliminate the bottleneck and duplication of work, and would promote efficient flow of the transactions and timely submission of the year-end reports to the Audit Team; and
Remind the officials concerned about the sanctions provided under Section 41 (3) of PD No. 1445.
The Municipal Accountant-Designate commented that there were times that she could not prepare schedules since the reports prepared by the Office of the Treasurer are not complete, however, she accepted the audit observation and committed to comply with the recommendations.
The Audit Team emphasized that subsidiary ledgers and schedules are required and should be submitted as part of the year-end reports.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. 	MSWDO to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer and the Heads of Office on the preparation of the GPB in accordance with the requirements of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, and No. 2016-01; and ensure that it is aligned with the GAD Agenda of the Municipality;
b. 	MSWDO personnel assigned in planning, implementation, and monitoring of GAD PAPs to undergo training on GAD, particularly training program on the GeRL Tool, and seek the help of a gender analysis specialist/expert to assist the Municipality in catalyzing and accelerating gender mainstreaming;
c. 	MSWDO official to ensure that the GPB and GAD AR are correctly prepared, supported with the required attachments, and attributed PAPs were subjected to Gender-Responsive LGU (GeRL) Ka Ba Self-Assessment Tool, GeRL tool tests, and other relevant tools; and
d. 	Accounting Office and Budget Office to integrate the RC codes in their respective systems.
The Municipal Budget Officer explained that the management has chosen to appropriate a separate fund for GAD instead of attributing its PAPs to GAD because there is a lot of requirements that should be complied when attribution is used.  According to him, the Municipality has a capacity to appropriate a separate fund, so the management did that instead of attribution of PAPs.  He added that the preparation of the supporting documents and test tools is an additional workload for him on top of the preparation of GAD PB, because during that time nobody is preparing it until the appointment of the new GAD Focal Person.
The Audit Team emphasized that attribution should be made because the PAPs contained in the GAD PB and AR are not sufficient to meet the 5% requirement.  With attribution, it would be more than enough.  The management needs only to train its GAD Focal Person to enable him to prepare the project proposal, HGDG test, GeRL Ka Ba Self-Assessment Tool, GAD PB, and AR, among others.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to investigate the composition of the
“unreconciled items” reflected in the bank reconciliation statements and
prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the deficiencies. Likewise,
require her to regularly prepare bank reconciliation statements, pursuant to
Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that the LCE:
1. Oblige the municipal officials and employees concerned to
liquidate the balances of their cash advances immediately;
2. Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to verify the
composition of the Advances to Officers and Employees account,
prepare the necessary journal entries to correct its balance and
the balances of other related accounts in the financial statements,
and see to it that no additional cash advances will be granted to
said municipal officials and employees unless they have fully
liquidated their cash advances.
3. Strictly enforce the pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA
Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of
cash advances.

We recommended that the LCE:
1. Cause the conduct of semestral physical inventory of supplies,
pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS for Local Government Units,
Volume I;
2. Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain and update Stock
Cards, and reconcile them with the Report on the Physical Count of
Inventories; and
3. Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to maintain and
update Supplies Ledger Cards; reconcile them with the Report on
the Physical Count of Inventories; adjust affected accounts and
records, if any; and submit the supporting documents for the
journal entry made to record the consumption of Welfare Goods
Inventory and for Drugs and Medicines Inventory.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant-
Designate to prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the deficiency,
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009, and Sections 111(2) and 112 of
Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal
Accountant-Designate and the Municipal Treasurer to verify the composition
of the beginning balances of the Due to BIR accounts under the General Fund,
Special Education Fund and Trust Fund, and remit immediately to BIR all
taxes withheld as of December 31, 2017.
Henceforth, remit to BIR taxes withheld from payments to
contractors/suppliers and from compensation of employees pursuant to the
pertinent provisions of Revenue Regulation Nos. 16-2005 and 17-2003.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant-
Designate and the Municipal Treasurer to verify the composition of the
beginning balances of the Due to GSIS accounts under the General Fund, and
remit immediately the amount of P25,326.96 due to GSIS as of December 31,
2017.
Henceforth, remit to GSIS the employees’ and government agency’s
contributions, as well as the loan amortizations promptly, pursuant to the
pertinent provisions of RA 8291 and its IRR.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly enforce compliance
with Section 6 of Annex E of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 in the implementation
of infrastructure projects, particularly on the withholding of 10% retention
money from the progress payments to contractors.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant-
Designate to require the contractors concerned to post the subject warranty
securities. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of
Notice/s of Disallowance in case of failure of the contractors concerned to post
the required warranty securities.
Henceforth, the Municipality should strictly enforce compliance with
the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR, and Section 9.1.1.4 of
COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the implementation of infrastructure projects,
particularly on the posting of warranty securities before final payment.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant-
Designate and the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the 26 paid
disbursement vouchers and related supporting documents. Henceforth,
strictly observe the timely submission of accounts pursuant to the pertinent
provisions of PD 1445, RA 7160 and other existing regulations.

We recommended that the LCE ensure that the 20% DF be fully
utilized as prescribed in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommended that the Local School Board, of which the LCE is the
Chairman, formulate a strategic prioritization policy for the allocation and
utilization of SEF to Projects, Programs, and Activities that are geared toward
building a sturdy foundation for quality education as enumerated in the DepEd-
DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017.
Moreover, encourage the claimants from the SEF to file their claims early, and
with complete supporting documents.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant be required to submit immediately the fifty one (51) disbursement vouchers, together with the supporting documents to support the recorded disbursements totaling P5,108,590.60. Henceforth, strictly observe the timely submission of accounts pursuant to Section 347 RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality refrain from charging the wages of Job Order personnel to the general services allocation, instead those hired should be included in the budget of implemented projects so that all programs will be attained.
observations.managementAction: 2015- Payments to Job Order personnel were charged to MOOE.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that wages of Job Order personnel be charged to MOOE funds. In no case shall their wages be charged against the allocation for personnel services.
observations.managementAction: 2014- The wages of Job Order personnel were charged to MOOE funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned officers and employees submit the lacking documents to support the disbursements for fuel expenses.
observations.managementAction: 2014- Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly implement the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184, Section 4 (6) of PD 1445, and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: 2014- Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should direct all Collectors and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 26 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992. We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjusting entries to come up with the correct balance of the Vault account.
observations.managementAction: 2013- The municipal treasurer issued a memo directing the collectors to remit their collections on a weekly basis. She also keeps on reminding them about this matter.
observations.reason: There were instances wherein the directive was not followed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should ensure that all executive officials and employees of the municipality faithfully discharge their duties and functions as provided for by regulations. We further recommend that management provide an inventory of land, land improvements and building and the results of the physical count be reconciled with its recorded valuation. Likewise, PPE Ledger Card and Real Property Card should be maintained for each category of property and land, respectively. These properties should be registered in the name of the Municipality with the Register of Deeds.
observations.managementAction: 2013- The management did not take steps to facilitate the titling though it has the Deed of Donation with the Certificate of Title in the name of the donor.
observations.reason: Some accounts previously dormant were transferred to current accounts and Public Infrastructure.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 13, 2013.
observations.managementAction: 2013- Some reports were submitted but were late.
observations.reason: Deadlines in the submission of reports were not strictly followed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should comply strictly with the provisions of Section 3, rule 18 of the IRR of Republic Act 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: 2013- Some actions were taken.
observations.reason: Please see discussion on Finding No. 6 this report

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provides documentary support and identify projects in the approved budget and AIProgram and ensure submission of disbursement vouchers and supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: 2013- Partially implemented
observations.reason: Non-observance of the existing rules.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly implement the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. This recommendation is without prejudice to issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Disallowance resulting from post-audit of transactions.
observations.managementAction: 2012- Some accomplishment reports and Job Order contracts were attached to the disbursements.
observations.reason: The provisions of the law were not strictly observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provides at least 5% of the total annual budget for Gender and Development (GAD) programs and projects and that the GAD Plan and Budget should be prepared and the disbursements thereof should address the purposes/policies of Gender and Development (GAD).
observations.managementAction: 2012- The management has no plan and has not established a GAD Focal Point System.
observations.reason: Management did not implement a GAD Focal Point System.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the accounting procedures, forms and format of reports pursuant to COA Circular 2006-002, dated January 31, 2006 and NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol. I.
observations.managementAction: 2012- Obligation Requests were numbered and dated. Some Disbursement Requests were not numbered and some were not dated.
observations.reason: Management did not implement all the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to record the LGU’s transactions in the journals and ledgers and to submit the same to COA for audit.
observations.managementAction: 2011- JEV, General Ledgers and Journals were already prepared and maintained.
observations.reason: Prior year balances do not have subsidiary records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be advised not to approve and disburse the funds of the government without satisfying the requirements set by the law. The LGU officials are advised to process transactions only if they are in conformity with the laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: 2010- Documentary requirements in AICS were complied, but in other types of disbursements complied were not met.
observations.reason: Absence of controls and/or non-observance of the same.

observations.recommendation: Enjoin the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to observe and implement the provisions of Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382. Instruct them to remit promptly all collections to the concerned government agencies where the funds rightfully belong.
observations.managementAction: 2010- The LGU is currently updating its remittances and is taking actions to comply with Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

observations.recommendation: We require the concerned accountable officers to immediately settle their suspensions and disallowances in accordance with the 2009 Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts.
